danger_signals.visual_form: Charger Alien (Warrior Xenomorph) sprites with distinctive hunched posture and elongated head
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; player must engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar visual reduction on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Player approaching vulnerable state; risk of defeat imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage-specific obstacles and environmental elements (waterfall basin in Stage 3, urban areas in Stage 1-2)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage or movement restriction zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Health drain during special attack execution; visual power bar depletion tied to player HP
danger_signals.meaning: Cost-benefit risk: powerful attacks damage the player; requires tactical consideration

safety_signals.visual_form: Predator character becomes translucent/invisible on screen
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability period; enemies cannot detect or attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Spear and disc weapons as collectible sprites; visual distinction from environment
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition enables enhanced offensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Cleared stage areas with no visible enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for movement and strategic positioning

faction_indicators.color_identity: Brown and blue (International version); Orange and green (Japanese version)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Predator palette changed between regions to reflect canonical color scheme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Predator occupies center of arena; dominates screen real estate during gameplay

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinctive elongated head crest, hunched attacking posture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Japanese version: Drone-type with horn; International version: Warrior Xenomorph with closed mouth (more canonical)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spawn from level architecture; appear as primary hostile entities throughout stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Predator sprite rendered in orange/green palette with aggressive fist-raised walking stance; Drone-type Aliens with horn protrusions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slower base movement speed requiring double-tap dash input; special attacks drain player health; cloaking lasts 44 seconds; weapons single-use per pickup

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Predator rendered in brown/blue palette with casual walking posture; Warrior Xenomorphs with closed mouths; Stage 3 redesigned as waterfall basin instead of urban area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant dashing movement speed as default; L/R slide input instead of dash-attack hybrid; special attacks cost no health; cloaking lasts 17 seconds; weapons have 6 uses per pickup

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power bar fills with color segments (gray, yellow, blue sections); bar flashes at variable rate depending on charge level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player movement restricted during charge; visual feedback indicates readiness threshold; full charge displays visual notification (flashing rate differs by region)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Predator sprite becomes translucent/fades from view
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies do not engage; player can move freely without threat; temporary tactical advantage window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy density decreases; fewer Alien sprites on screen; clear pathways emerge in arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced combat pressure; opportunity for item collection and health management

environmental_storytelling.example: Stage 3 waterfall basin (International) or urban outdoor area (Japanese) establishes environment as contested territory; visual emphasis on natural/artificial boundaries suggests warfare in populated zones
Redesigned pods in International version to match Alien film series aesthetic; visual authenticity grounds the Xenomorph threat in recognizable franchise canon
Japanese spear rendered as dangerous/impractical (hazardous to wielder); International spear redesigned with ergonomic grip; visual representation of difficulty/accessibility philosophy
Special attack power bar visual structure (segment sizing, flashing rate) communicates different difficulty parameters between regions without text

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Japanese: aggressive fist-raised stance (ready posture); International: casual walking stance (relaxed posture)
Japanese version requires double-tap input to dash; International version moves at constant dash speed
Japanese: laser attack drains 5-40 HP depending on intensity; International: laser attack costs no health (pure benefit)
Spawns from level geometry; pursues Predator player character; hunched posture indicates aggressive intent
Visually distinct with horn protrusion; identical combat behavior to Warrior variant; replaced in International versions for canonical accuracy
Predator becomes translucent; enemies cease pursuit; visual stealth indicator

notes: This SNES port is a single-character beat-em-up (Predator only) with asymmetric regional balance: Japanese version emphasizes risk-reward (health-draining powers, single-use weapons, slower movement) while International release prioritizes accessibility (free special attacks, multi-use weapons, constant sprint). Visual design changes between regions—Predator color scheme, Alien enemy types, Stage 3 environment, even ending credits text ('Dr. Banana' vs. 'K. Nakabayashi')—suggest Nintendo of America/Europe content filtering and localization philosophy. Unused 2P versus mode (Predator vs. Charger Alien) hints at original design scope before streamlining. Danger/safety communication relies on sprite presence/absence, item pickups, and HUD bar depletion rather than atmospheric lighting or camera work.
